text: MISSING SUFFIX: Many organisation entities follow the pattern '<Name> <BusinessSectorWord>' with NO GmbH/AG/KG suffix at all. These are completely uncovered.  The suffix is one of these known business sectors: Versicherung, Touristik, E-Commerce, Beratung, Handel, Recycling.

This rule has a critical bug: the negative lookbehind (?<![a-zA-Z\s]) blocks every match that appears after a space, i.e. almost every mid-sentence entity. Replace the lookbehind with a plain \b word boundary at the start. Also add OG and KG to the suffix alternatives. Suggested fix: \b[A-ZÄÖÜ][A-Za-zÄÖÜäöüß\-]*(?:\s+[A-ZÄÖÜ][A-Za-zÄÖÜäöüß\-]*)*\s+(?:GmbH|GMBH|GmbH\s*&\s*Co\s*KG|AG|KG|OG)\b

The location pattern should allow digits and forward slashes as well as letters and hyphens. Also ensure it still matches 'FA Tirol Ost', 'FA Spittal Villach' (two-word locations) and 'FA Salzburg-Stadt' (hyphenated). The rule should also match when written as 'Finanzamt' (full form) with the same flexible location pattern — consider merging both into one rule.

This rule hard-codes specific entity names from the training set. This is fragile — it will not generalise to unseen entities. Keep only entities that cannot be covered by the general GmbH/AG/Finanzamt/sector rules (e.g. truly irregular names). Remove entries that are already covered by the other rules to reduce redundancy.
